role: Former Foreign Ministry Deputy for Political Affairs; 1979 embassy hostage-taker
bio: Hossein Sheikholeslam was one of the student leaders who seized the US Embassy in Tehran on November 4, 1979, participating in the [Iran hostage crisis](/events/iran-hostage-crisis-1979). Following the revolution, he rose through the Foreign Ministry to serve 17 years as Deputy Foreign Minister for Political Affairs — one of the most influential career positions in Iranian diplomacy. He was ambassador to Syria from 1998 to 2003, a period during which [Hezbollah](/orgs/hezbollah) expanded its operations under Iranian direction. Sheikholeslam is credited by researchers as one of the figures involved in the co-founding of Lebanese Hezbollah in the early 1980s, coordinating between the IRGC and Lebanese Shia militias in the Bekaa Valley. He was an ally of [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) and a consistent advocate for the resistance axis. He died of COVID-19 on March 26, 2020.
editorial_notes: Died March 26 2020 of COVID-19. 17 years as FM Deputy for Political Affairs. Co-founding role in Hezbollah per multiple researchers.
